stacked_statement:
Adopting this stack lets CuraOS IdM beat each source 1:1 and beat all of them stacked, while staying generator-first, security-weighted, and person/tenant-centric.

ONE-TO-ONE (we beat each source on its own turf):
- vs node-oidc-provider (panva): we get its FAPI-certified AS protocol surface verbatim, but mount it generator-first behind a Drizzle+Citus tenant-scoped adapter and run it as a Node sidecar (identity-oidc-service) so the Bun-primary plane is preserved. Source gives a single-instance, single-DB engine; we add per-tenant issuer routing + codegen adapter the source never ships.
- vs Logto/Zitadel (full CIAM): they hand you an opaque schema + second runtime + flat org-RBAC template. We keep Drizzle-owned identity data, per-org role divergence via @curaos/policy, Citus DB-per-tenant isolation, and PHI inside overlay schemas. We take their org/JIT/invite UX as a reference design, not a black box.
- vs @simplewebauthn: already in-tree at v13.3.1; we bump to v13.3.2 and wrap it in a generated PasskeyModule with per-tenant rpID (eTLD+1) + Related Origin Requests for BYO-domain tenants. Source is a library; we make it tenant-aware and generator-emitted.
- vs jose/openid-client/oauth4webapi (panva): shared in-tree dep graph (jose already pinned). We get FAPI-2.0 RP-certified primitives and emit SET/RISC/CAEP builders + DPoP guards from codegen rather than hand-wiring per service.
- vs ldapts/SCIMMY/SCIMGateway: each is a niche leaf; we wrap them in generated, Filter.escape-safe, bearer-guarded, TLS-1.3-tested, Postgres-plugin (never LokiJS) modules with mandatory tenant-scoped tokens the libraries leave to the caller.
- vs OpenFGA: we take Zanzibar graph traversal for the cross-resource B2B-org-inheritance cases ONLY, pinned >=v1.17.1 + Helm-hardened, while @curaos/policy stays the primary in-process RBAC/ABAC plane (zero CVE cadence, no sidecar) for the 80% case.
- vs Ory Polis / Better Auth / SuperTokens / Cerbos-WASM / SpiceDB / immudb: rejected or referenced because each fails a binding CuraOS constraint (better-sqlite3+TypeORM Bun blocker, global-email-uniqueness wontfix, EE paywall on self-hosted IdP role, Hub-gated air-gap break, ~2-month correctness-advisory cadence, BSL redistribution). We harvest their DESIGN (org model, flow state machine, tamper-proof schema, policy DSL) without inheriting their runtime/license poison.

STACKED (we beat the union of all sources):
No single source gives you: (a) a FAPI-certified AS, (b) Drizzle+Citus DB-per-tenant identity data ownership, (c) Bun-primary runtime, (d) passkey-first WebAuthn with per-tenant rpID + ROR, (e) one authorization plane (@curaos/policy) with OpenFGA only for graph cases, (f) HMAC-keyed app-layer audit chain + pgaudit DB-layer + optional immudb high-assurance anchor, (g) person-centric dual surfaces (patient/customer self-service + clinician/admin management) over the SAME contract, and (h) every artifact emitted from a single TypeSpec/codegen toolchain so the trio (core/personal/business + healthstack overlay) stays symmetric. Each source covers a slice and forces a runtime, schema, or license compromise on the rest. CuraOS composes the certified protocol cores + audited crypto primitives we IMPORT with the tenancy, policy, audit, and person-centric surfaces we OWN and GENERATE - so we inherit the security pedigree of the best-in-class engines while keeping the data model, runtime, and journey design under our own generator. The net: every required management/compliance/back-office feature is preserved (no_loss), every capability gets a person-facing journey surface AND a management surface from one data+contract spine, and the whole IdM plane is reproducible from codegen rather than hand-assembled from nine incompatible vendors.

register.name: node-oidc-provider (oidc-provider)
register.what: OpenID Certified OAuth2/OIDC Authorization Server library; token lifecycle, auth-code/device/CIBA, JWKS, PKCE/DPoP/PAR/JAR/JARM/FAPI. The engine Logto runs on.
register.stack_fit: Koa-based, ESM-only; NestJS via nest-oidc-provider v3 (NestJS>=11). BUN BLOCKER: maintainer refuses non-Node runtimes; run as Node sidecar (identity-oidc-service). Custom Drizzle adapter required (7-method interface).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Pure self-hosted library; air-gap ready; BYO Drizzle storage adapter.
register.security_notes: FAPI 2.0 + 10 other profiles certified. No direct core CVEs. v9.8.x active fix cadence (HTML-injection, SSRF dispatcher patched). Solo maintainer (panva) bus-factor; no third-party pentest. Highest-value attack surface; pin exact + vendor/fork-ready.
register.generator_first_target: Drizzle adapter class (per-tenant schema binding); NestJS OidcModule config factory (tenant-resolver -> provider config); client-registration seeds, JWKS rotation job, consent/interaction page stubs.

register.name: panva/jose
register.what: JWA/JWS/JWE/JWT/JWK/JWKS primitives; DPoP proof validation, access-token signing, JWKS endpoint, refresh-token encryption. Crypto foundation for the AS and the identity-service.
register.stack_fit: First-class Bun support; ESM-only handled transparently by Bun. ALREADY in identity-service tree at v6.2.3. Pure crypto, Drizzle/Zod-orthogonal.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Local library; createRemoteJWKSet is the only opt-in external call.
register.security_notes: Snyk 0 vulns v6.2.3, 63M weekly downloads. Zero deps = minimal supply-chain. panva is Node JOSE reference implementer. All historical CVEs are DoS on niche algos, all patched; no token-forgery CVE ever.
register.generator_first_target: Key-generation bootstrap (JWKS init at start); JWKS rotation job emitter; DPoP proof verifier in @curaos/auth-sdk; SET/RISC/CAEP token builder.

register.name: nest-oidc-provider
register.what: NestJS DI wrapper around node-oidc-provider: OidcModule.forRootAsync(), @InjectOidcProvider(), createAdapterFactory hook.
register.stack_fit: NestJS 11 + oidc-provider v9. Inherits the Bun blocker from oidc-provider. Community single-maintainer (adrianbrs, 77 stars, 225 weekly dl); thin enough to fork. Multi-tenant isolation undocumented - must validate no cross-tenant state bleed.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Library, in-process with identity-oidc-service.
register.security_notes: Zero crypto surface; security delegated to oidc-provider. Snyk 0 CVEs across 15 versions. Socket.dev unverified (run socket scan before merge). Singleton DI default - per-tenant config isolation unproven.
register.generator_first_target: OidcModule.forRootAsync factory codegen: tenant-scoped provider config + Drizzle adapter injection + JWKS key loading.

register.name: Logto (self-host-service mode)
register.what: Full TS/Node CIAM (OIDC+OAuth2.1+SAML), organizations, RBAC, SSO, MFA, passkeys, admin console, hosted sign-in. Built on node-oidc-provider.
register.stack_fit: Node/Koa service + own opaque Postgres schema (own migrator, NOT Drizzle). Conflicts structurally with Citus distributed tables - dual-PG fleet required for the most critical service. Custom claim scripts run in-process with server privileges on self-hosted.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Self-hosted only (air-gap via Docker, --disable HIBP check); Logto Cloud never used.
register.security_notes: SOC2 Type II (Cloud scope, not self-hosted). Inherits oidc-provider OIDC certs but Logto wrapper layer is uncertified. No self-hosted pentest. In-process claim scripts = multi-tenant privilege risk.
register.generator_first_target: REFERENCE: org-template RBAC + JIT provisioning + invite flow design for the identity-service org module. Do not adopt as runtime dep.

register.name: @simplewebauthn/server + @simplewebauthn/browser
register.what: WebAuthn RP library: registration/authentication ceremonies, MDS3 MetadataService, attestation formats, conditional UI. Passkey engine.
register.stack_fit: ALREADY in identity-service at v13.3.1; live in webauthn-*.service.ts + auth.controller.ts. globalThis.crypto-first = correct Bun path. expectedRPID/expectedOrigin accept arrays for per-tenant allowlisting.

register.security_notes: 160 FIDO conformance tests passing. Only GHSA is Low x5c self-signed-root bypass, fixed v13.3.2 - MANDATORY bump before passkey attestation goes live. CVE-2026-13029 (Chrome) and CVE-2025-26788 (StrongKey) do not apply.
register.generator_first_target: PasskeyModule/PasskeyService/DrizzlePasskeyRepository/PasskeyController parameterized per tenant; rpID=eTLD+1 + Related Origin Requests for BYO-domain tenants; MetadataService bootstrap at app module.

register.name: otpauth (hectorm)
register.what: HOTP (RFC 4226) + TOTP (RFC 6238) with QR URI generation, multi-runtime. TOTP-MFA enroll/verify, authenticator-app provisioning.
register.stack_fit: Bun-native ESM. Zero prod deps beyond @noble/hashes + @scure/base. new TOTP({secret}).validate({token,window:1}). QR rendering needs separate qrcode/sharp.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Pure in-process; air-gap safe.
register.security_notes: Historical single-digit auth bypass fixed 3.2.8 (2020); v9.5.1 clean (Snyk 0). Shai-Hulud worm did NOT include otpauth; SLSA L2 provenance. MUST verify timingSafeEqual in v9.5.1 dist + add replay guard (last-used counter per user/tenant) before adopt-final.
register.generator_first_target: TotpModule/TotpService/TotpController + MfaSettings Drizzle schema (last_totp_used_at, last_totp_window_counter) + replay-guard interceptor; TOTP gated as passkey fallback only.

register.stack_fit: Requires explicit plugin wiring (@otplib/plugin-crypto-noble + plugin-base32-scure) for audited path - a footgun. ~2.3MB unpacked. No NestJS examples. Bus-factor 1.
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Fully in-process; air-gap safe.
register.security_notes: Snyk 0 on v13.4.1; transitive devDep patches signal non-zero supply-chain surface for what is 15 lines of HMAC-SHA1. Plugin-must-be-selected footgun.
register.generator_first_target: Same emitter as otpauth; reference only if both rejected in favor of 30-line in-tree totp.ts on Bun Web Crypto.

register.what: Audited hash/MAC primitives: SHA-2/3, HMAC, HKDF, PBKDF2, Scrypt, Argon2.
register.stack_fit: NOT in tree. Tree already has @node-rs/argon2 (p=4 native), jose, Bun-native node:crypto hkdf - all three claimed use-cases already covered.

register.security_notes: Cure53 Jan-2022 audit EXCLUDED Argon2/blake3/sha3-addons/sha1. noble docs warn JS Argon2 is 5x slower than native (attacker advantage) - functional regression vs in-tree @node-rs/argon2 for recovery-code hashing.

register.what: MFA backup codes: CSPRNG-generated, Argon2id-hashed, single-use, Drizzle-stored with used_at.
register.stack_fit: ~20 lines using crypto.randomBytes + @node-rs/argon2 (already in tree). Drizzle recovery_codes(user_id, code_hash, used_at, tenant_id).
register.local_vs_3rdparty: Fully in-process; air-gap safe.
register.security_notes: Security = CSPRNG (not Math.random) + Argon2id before storage + single-use enforcement + rate limit + audit event. Shown once on enrollment.
register.generator_first_target: RecoveryCodeModule: generate (returns once, stores hashes), verify (marks used_at), regenerate (invalidate all) in identity-service.

Tight Ory-ecosystem coupling conflicts with CuraOS building its own IdP; weaker consistency than SpiceDB; no formal audit. OpenFGA covers the ReBAC lane without the coupling.

AGPL-3.0 (can run as service but not fork/embed without open-sourcing CuraOS); FusionAuth acquisition Nov-2025 adds roadmap uncertainty. Reference its tenant-scoped schema DSL only; OpenFGA is the primary.
Cloud-SaaS-only, OSS library abandoned - fails self-hosted-first + air-gap charter. Highest vendor lock-in. Reject.

OIDC/OAuth2 AS role (the one job needed - replace pocket-id) is NOT available self-hosted at any tier (managed-only); MFA/TOTP/multi-tenancy/M2M are paid EE (violates self-hosted-first); no Bun support; no Drizzle schema ownership; sidecar Java/Go process; no independent audit for an IdP role.

10+ advisories in ~18mo (CVE-2025-61928 9.3 account takeover, passkey IDOR, 2FA bypass, open-redirect) - auth-bypass logic in unaudited plugins; not an IdP (it's an embedded-app auth framework); excluding the CVE-heavy enterprise plugins leaves only password+email flows we already have. Reject for IdP use; reference for invitation UX.

DB conflict is blocking: internal-migrator-owned schema cannot coexist with Citus distributed tables (dual-PG fleet for the most critical service is operationally untenable for self-hosted-first/air-gap); simpler answer already in-tree (jose + @simplewebauthn + node-oidc-provider give the same cert lineage with Drizzle schema ownership); in-process custom-claim execution is a multi-tenant privilege regression.

License claim FALSE - URLs resolve to www.shadcn.io, a third-party COMMERCIAL site explicitly unaffiliated with official shadcn/ui; the named blocks do not exist in MIT ui.shadcn.com; registry-injection attack vector (July 2025). Build the five blocks as generator templates against owned Radix primitives + TanStack Table.

Cloud-SaaS-only (CrowdStrike-acquired) - violates self-hosted-first + air-gap. Strongest tamper-proof guarantee (Merkle + Arweave) but incompatible runtime. Possible BYO-3rdparty audit-sink adapter interface only; not core.

Single maintainer, 0 stars/forks, 6 commits, v0.1.0, no provenance - exact npm supply-chain-attack profile on a security-critical surface; HMAC linear chain only (no Merkle/anchor); self-referencing evidence. Implement the 8-line hash chain in @curaos/audit-sdk (seq/hash_prev/hash already modeled per ADR-0157); adopt immudb if Merkle/anchor needed.

coverage_gaps.capability: FAPI-certified OIDC Authorization Server runnable Bun-native in-process
coverage_gaps.why_no_option: The only FAPI-2.0-capable Node AS (node-oidc-provider) is maintainer-locked to Node (node:http coupling, v9.8.4 actively detects+rejects non-Node). No Bun-native certified AS exists. Build-from-scratch on jose is 3-6 months re-implementing the most security-critical component. RESOLUTION: run node-oidc-provider as a Node sidecar (not from scratch) - the gap is runtime placement, not a missing engine. Only the Drizzle+Citus tenant-scoped adapter is genuine build-from-scratch (justified: no community Drizzle adapter exists, and multi-tenant Citus routing is CuraOS-specific).

node-oidc-provider has no native one-provider-per-tenant support; SpiceDB/OpenFGA/Logto all have no multi-tenant isolation that composes with Citus DB-per-tenant. Build-from-scratch justified for the tenant-routing layer + per-tenant Provider lifecycle (memory/startup scales linearly - a CuraOS architecture decision no library encodes).

coverage_gaps.capability: HMAC-keyed, tenant-scoped, generator-emitted app-layer audit chain
coverage_gaps.why_no_option: Every importable option (nestjs-audit-trail, tamper-evident-log) ships an UNKEYED chain (compromised DB write role recomputes it = zero integrity) or is supply-chain-untrustworthy. Build-from-scratch justified and small: ~90 lines (or 8 lines into existing @curaos/audit-sdk per ADR-0157) using node:crypto createHmac + key from @curaos/secrets + write-only PG role. The schema (seq/hash_prev/hash) already exists; only the keyed computation is missing.

coverage_gaps.capability: Self-service flow engine that owns NO schema and composes with @curaos/tenancy
coverage_gaps.why_no_option: Better Auth / SuperTokens / Logto / Ory Kratos all OWN an opaque schema and an org/membership table set that collides with @curaos/tenancy + the global-email-uniqueness constraint (wontfix in Better Auth) breaks multi-tenant CIAM. Build-from-scratch justified: self-service flows (registration/recovery/verification/magic-link/progressive-profiling) are NestJS handlers + Drizzle queries over the EXISTING identity-service schema - no framework can express CuraOS's tenant-scoped person-centric model without schema conflict.

coverage_gaps.capability: Person-facing + management dual-surface admin UI from one contract
coverage_gaps.why_no_option: shadcn-admin-kit/react-admin/Logto-console all impose a vendor dataProvider contract OR a non-importable monolith console, and none model the person/tenant-centric dual surface (same data, two re-centered experiences) from the lens. Build-from-scratch justified using already-installed primitives (Radix + TanStack Table/Query + RHF + Zod) emitted from @curaos/policy + TypeSpec - the dual-surface re-centering is a CuraOS design constraint no admin framework encodes.

coverage_gaps.capability: Air-gap-clean tamper-PROOF (Merkle + external anchor) high-assurance audit for break-glass
coverage_gaps.why_no_option: Strongest options are cloud-only (Pangea/Arweave - violates air-gap) or BSL-restricted redistribution (immudb server). app-layer HMAC chain + pgaudit DB-layer is adequate for SOC2/HIPAA application tier. Genuine gap only for the highest-assurance Merkle-proof tier - DEFER: adopt self-hosted immudb as a complementary DB-layer control if/when a HIPAA tamper-PROOF (not tamper-evident) requirement is explicitly confirmed; do not build a Merkle engine speculatively (YAGNI).
